mission.title: thanos: Research groupcache as alternative cache client to memcached
mission.description: Research groupcache as alternative cache client to memcached. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current thanos deployment
steps.description:
Verify your thanos version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
helm list -n thanos 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working thanos installation.

steps.title: Review thanos configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get configmap -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=thanos
```
I think something which would easy up Thanos deployment with cache is https://github.com/golang/groupcache This is project done my Memcached author and it's replicated in mem cache.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Research groupcache as alternative cache client to memcached
steps.description:
My semi-working branch: https://github.com/GiedriusS/thanos/tree/experiment/groupcache_caching_bucket.

Some thoughts after working on this:

There is no way to remove a group after it has been registered with groupcache without changing groupcache's code itself. This means that if we were to use
```yaml
2021/06/23 07:15:12 http: Accept error: accept tcp [::]:9091: accept4: too many open files; retrying in 10ms
2021/06/23 07:15:12 http: Accept error: accept tcp [::]:9091: accept4: too many open files; retrying in 20ms
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get events -n thanos --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Research groupcache as alternative cache client to memcached" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
My semi-working branch: https://github.com/GiedriusS/thanos/tree/experiment/groupcache_caching_bucket.

Some thoughts after working on this:

There is no way to remove a group after it has been registered with groupcache without changing groupcache's code itself.
codeSnippets:
2021/06/23 07:15:12 http: Accept error: accept tcp [::]:9091: accept4: too many open files; retrying in 10ms
2021/06/23 07:15:12 http: Accept error: accept tcp [::]:9091: accept4: too many open files; retrying in 20ms

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with thanos installed or the issue environment reproducible.
